EpiGraph PH

Philippines HIV surveillance atlas

Curated epidemiologic evidence from the HIV/STI corpus, centered on national targets, regional inequality, long-run burden, and key populations.

Mode: booting
Updated: waiting
Scope: hiv_sti
National Cascade
Official target board and quarterly cascade
Loading

Annual UNAIDS context and quarterly SHIP/WHO surveillance are separated visually. The board keeps the official third-95 limitation explicit rather than smoothing it away.

Regional Inequality
Observed yearly regional stage matrix
Loading

The publication matrix is the cross-sectional regional view. It keeps only observed yearly coverage and encodes distance to the 95 target without reverting to the old ladder chart.

Anomalies
Performance versus burden
Loading

Underperformance and treatment leakage are treated as one decision problem. The publication board shows both the deviation from expected cascade performance and the measured burden of loss from care.

Historical View
National burden and annual context
Loading

Only the strongest historical series remain in the Overview. Weak or sparse panels were removed so the annual burden story stays readable and defensible.

Key Populations
Sentinel subgroups with observed windows
Loading

The sentinel board keeps subgroup windows explicit. Missing years remain visible as missing rather than being interpolated into a fake continuous series.

Regional Explorer

Yearly regional comparison

Loading

Observed yearly regional cascade only. Use the matrix for cross-sectional comparison, the fingerprint for one region over time, and the bump chart for rank movement on a selected stage.

Regional stage matrix

Rows are regions and columns are the three cascade stages for the selected year. Hover cells for exact values, sources, and report periods.

Selected region fingerprint

Current values, gap to target, year-over-year movement, and the latest source report for the selected region.

Regional rank change

Regions are ranked by the selected cascade stage for each observed year. The selected region is highlighted; all others remain as muted context.

Anomaly Explorer

Performance versus treatment burden

Loading

A unified yearly view. Negative x-values are below the fitted regional expectation, while higher y-values indicate a larger share of documented loss from care. Point size shows absolute leakage burden.

Performance versus burden quadrant

Hover points to inspect residual stage, leakage burden, and source references for the selected year.

Methods

How each figure was generated

Loading

Each section documents the figure question, exact formula block, coverage window, estimation policy, source precedence, and source table for the corresponding publication figure. The publication PDFs include the same methodological detail plus the vector figure export.

Sources

Source reports, datasets, and checkpoints

Loading

This is the source inventory behind the current figures. It includes official international datasets, official Philippine surveillance reports, and the local source reports used for directly extracted derived series.

Experimental estimates

Observed-only atlas, synthetic layer withheld

Observed only

This tab is reserved for estimated regional backfill. No synthetic values are published into the current atlas. Estimated series will only appear here once they are constrained by official national totals and clearly marked as modeled rather than observed.

Current status

The official atlas remains observed-only. Regional observed yearly coverage currently starts in 2024 for cascade coverage and in 2023 for yearly leakage.

  • Not published: synthetic pre-2024 regional cascade values
  • Not published: uncertainty ribbons or modeled regional trajectories
  • Why: older official region-level denominators remain incomplete, so injecting estimates into the official atlas would weaken the evidence standard

Planned model guardrails

When this layer is turned on, it will remain separate from the official overview and explorer.

  • Model family: yearly hierarchical partial-pooling fit with region intercepts and slopes
  • Constraint: estimates must remain coherent with official national totals and observed regional anchors
  • Display policy: dashed estimated lines, shaded uncertainty, and explicit “Estimated, not observed” labels on every figure
